_disclaimer: Facility locations are approximate (city-level accuracy). Capacity figures are publicly reported estimates and may not reflect current state. Sources listed per entry.

data_centers.name: Equinix Ashburn VA Campus (IAD)
data_centers.notes: Ashburn, VA — the world's largest internet exchange point. Equinix operates 15+ data centers in Loudoun County.

data_centers.name: Digital Realty Northern Virginia (IAD)
data_centers.notes: Major hyperscale campus in Ashburn/Sterling, VA. Digital Realty is one of the largest campus operators in Northern Virginia.

data_centers.name: Google The Dalles Campus (Oregon)
data_centers.notes: Google's first owned data center, continuously expanded. Leverages Columbia River hydropower and Oregon enterprise zone exemptions.

data_centers.name: Amazon AWS New Albany Campus (Ohio)
data_centers.notes: One of AWS's largest global campuses. Licking County / New Albany area hosts multiple AWS data center buildings totaling millions of sq ft.

data_centers.name: Google Council Bluffs Campus (Iowa)
data_centers.notes: Google's first Iowa data center. Expands regularly; benefits from Iowa's 0% equipment sales tax and wind energy.

data_centers.name: Microsoft West Des Moines Campus (Iowa)
data_centers.notes: Major Microsoft Azure campus in West Des Moines (Polk County). Iowa's 0% data center equipment tax applies.

data_centers.name: Switch SUPERNAP Campus (Tahoe Reno)
data_centers.notes: Tahoe Reno Industrial Center, Storey County NV. One of the world's largest data center campuses. Nevada tax abatements apply.

data_centers.name: Google Midlothian Campus (Texas)
data_centers.notes: Major Google data center campus in Midlothian, Ellis County TX. Benefits from Chapter 312 property tax abatements.

data_centers.name: xAI Colossus Supercomputer (Memphis, TN)
data_centers.notes: World's largest known GPU cluster as of late 2024 (100,000+ H100/H200 GPUs). Phase 2 expansion to 1M GPUs announced.

sources.label: xAI Colossus Memphis — Data Center Dynamics

data_centers.name: Apple Maiden Data Center (North Carolina)
data_centers.notes: Apple's first owned data center, Maiden, Catawba County NC. 100% renewable powered. Expanded multiple times.

sources.label: Apple Maiden Data Center — Data Center Knowledge

data_centers.name: Meta Altoona Data Center (Iowa)
data_centers.notes: Meta's first Iowa data center. Now one of its largest global facilities. 100% renewable powered via wind PPAs.

data_centers.name: Amazon AWS Boardman Campus (Oregon)
data_centers.notes: AWS US West (Oregon) Region anchor campus in Boardman, Morrow County. Multiple buildings; cheap Columbia River hydropower.

data_centers.name: CyrusOne Dallas (DFW Campus)
data_centers.notes: CyrusOne's flagship Dallas campus serves major enterprise and cloud customers. Multiple buildings in Irving/Carrollton area.

data_centers.name: Google Lenoir Data Center (North Carolina)
data_centers.notes: Google's Lenoir, NC campus (Caldwell County). Repurposed former furniture factory site; 100% renewable. Expanded multiple times.

sources.label: Google Lenoir North Carolina Data Center

data_centers.name: QTS Suwanee Campus (Atlanta Metro, GA)
data_centers.notes: QTS Suwanee, Gwinnett County GA. Major colocation hub for the Atlanta metro market. Georgia data center tax exemptions apply.

data_centers.name: Meta Prineville Campus (Oregon)
data_centers.notes: Meta's Prineville, OR campus (Crook County) — one of its first purpose-built data centers. Oregon enterprise zone and renewable hydro/wind.

data_centers.name: Google Berkeley County Data Center (SC)
data_centers.notes: Google's Berkeley County, SC campus in Goose Creek. South Carolina's 20-year tax exemptions apply. Expanded to one of Google's largest East Coast sites.

sources.label: Google Berkeley County South Carolina Data Center

data_centers.name: Microsoft Azure East US (Boydton, VA)
data_centers.notes: Microsoft's Boydton, VA campus (Mecklenburg County VA) anchors the Azure East US region. Very large multi-building campus.

data_centers.name: Iron Mountain Northern Virginia (Manassas)
data_centers.notes: Iron Mountain's Manassas campus (Prince William County VA). Part of the broader Nova data center market.

data_centers.name: Google Newton County Data Center (Covington, GA)
data_centers.notes: Google's Covington, GA campus (Newton County). One of Google's original US data centers; expanded continuously.

data_centers.name: Stargate Texas Campus (Abilene, TX) — Planned
data_centers.notes: One of multiple Stargate AI infrastructure sites in Texas. The Abilene/Taylor County location is the primary announced Texas site.

data_centers.name: Microsoft Mount Pleasant AI Campus (Wisconsin) — Planned
data_centers.notes: Microsoft's announced $3.3B AI data center campus in Mount Pleasant, Racine County WI. Located on former Foxconn site.

data_centers.name: Meta Aiken Campus (South Carolina) — Planned
data_centers.notes: Meta's announced data center campus in Aiken County, SC. SC's 20-year tax exemptions apply.

data_centers.name: Stargate Ohio Campus — Planned
data_centers.notes: Project Stargate Ohio site, planned for Columbus metro (Franklin County). Part of the $500B national AI infrastructure initiative.

data_centers.name: CoreWeave Chicago Metro Campus — Planned
data_centers.notes: CoreWeave's planned large-scale GPU cluster campus in the Chicago metro (Will County, IL). CoreWeave has rapidly expanded its US hyperscale AI infrastructure.

data_centers.name: Meta Richland Parish Campus (Louisiana) — Planned
data_centers.notes: Meta's announced $800M+ data center in Richland Parish, northeastern Louisiana. Leverages state ITEP tax incentives and lower energy costs.

data_centers.name: Amazon Morrow County Exascale Campus (Oregon) — Planned
data_centers.notes: AWS expansion in Morrow County (Boardman area), part of AWS's exascale-class Oregon campus buildout. Multiple facilities under development.

sources.label: AWS Oregon expansion — Data Center Dynamics

data_centers.name: Stargate Wisconsin Campus — Planned
data_centers.notes: One of the announced Stargate sites in Wisconsin (Waukesha County area). Part of the multi-state AI infrastructure buildout.

ai_campuses.name: xAI Colossus Supercomputer (Memphis, TN)
ai_campuses.notes: World's largest known GPU cluster (100,000+ H100/H200 GPUs as of 2024). Used for training Grok models. Phase 2 expanding to 1M GPUs.

ai_campuses.name: AWS Project Rainier (Licking County, OH)
ai_campuses.notes: Amazon's announced dedicated AI training cluster ('Project Rainier') featuring custom Trainium chips, co-located with the New Albany Ohio campus. Announced Sep 2024.

ai_campuses.name: Microsoft AI Supercomputer Campus (Racine Co, WI) — Planned
ai_campuses.notes: Microsoft's Mount Pleasant, WI campus is specifically designed for AI workloads. Part of the $3.3B Racine County investment announced May 2024.

ai_campuses.name: Meta AI Research (Menlo Park, CA)
ai_campuses.notes: Meta's Fundamental AI Research (FAIR) lab and AI training infrastructure in Menlo Park, San Mateo County CA. Home to LLaMA model development.

ai_campuses.name: OpenAI / Stargate Supercluster (Texas)
ai_campuses.notes: OpenAI's planned dedicated AI training supercluster in Texas, part of the $500B Project Stargate initiative. Largest announced AI compute investment in US history.

power_infrastructure.name: Dominion Energy Loudoun Substation (VA)
sources: Dominion Energy Northern Virginia transmission infrastructure

power_infrastructure.name: AEP Ohio Licking Valley Transmission (OH)
sources: AEP Ohio New Albany data center interconnection

sources.label: Bonneville Power Administration — The Dalles Dam

power_infrastructure.name: NV Energy Harry Allen Generation Station (NV)

sources.label: NV Energy Harry Allen Generation Station

power_infrastructure.name: TVA Allen Combined Cycle Plant (Memphis, TN)

fiber_network.name: Zayo Northern Virginia Dark Fiber Ring
